frameworks.name: multivariate pattern analysis (MVPA)
frameworks.one_line: decoding multivariate patterns from functional neuroimaging data

frameworks.name: hierarchical within-subject decoding
frameworks.one_line: standard two-step group MVPA combining within-subject results

core_question: How do hierarchical within-subject and inter-subject group-level MVPA schemes compare in sensitivity and interpretability?
key_assumptions: multivariate effects are statistically detectable at group level
inter-individual variability can be modelled parametrically
leave-one-subject-out cross-validation yields valid group inference
manipulations: size of the multivariate effect in simulated data
amount of inter-individual variability in simulated data
dataset type (artificial vs real fMRI, 15 and 39 subjects)
key_findings: the two schemes uncover distinct significant regions with only partial overlap
inter-subject pattern analysis detects smaller effects than the hierarchical scheme
inter-subject analysis facilitates interpretation of group-level results
interpretations: inter-subject pattern analysis is a straightforward and more powerful alternative for group-level MVPA
